expectations.text: Response demonstrates n() combined with .scale("C:minor") or similar
Response explains that n() numbers are scale degrees where 0 is the root
expectations.evidence: 'Numbers in n() are scale degrees: 0 = root, 1 = 2nd degree, 2 = 3rd degree, etc.'
.scale("C:minor") appears multiple times in code examples

expectations.text: Response indicates that any integer can be used and it will stay in scale
expectations.evidence: 'Any integer you use — positive, negative, or large — will stay within the scale, with octave shifts applied automatically'
